tool: Siril 1.4.4 seqstat (per-frame) + rej-average master, on CFA (undebayered) FITS

acquisition.vs_lights: darks PRECEDE set-01 lights (22:48:48..23:32:12) by ~4.5 min; darks span the camera warm-up phase

caveats: CFA-sampled (undebayered); 16-bit ADU scale; relative trends valid
arm base rig; a property of the data, transfers to x86

dark_level_pedestal_ADU.verdict: FLAT — every dark subtracts the same level

noise_sigma_ADU.pattern: monotonic rise, not plateaued = sensor self-heating over the 25-min run

light_leak_check.verdict: CAP FULLY ON — uniform pedestal, no gradient, no light tail, no amp-glow corner

cull_question.recommendation: USE ALL 214. Flat pedestal = all darks subtract fairly; a 100-frame master injects ~53% more noise (0.45->0.69 ADU) than the full set, to address a ~0.1 ADU pedestal diff = net WORSE. N beats the small thermal drift. Last darks match lights' temp best, but the flat pedestal makes all fair.
